message:
Fix update-cli to warn instead of fail when no checksum available

Bug fix: The update-cli command was throwing an error if no SHA256SUMS.txt
or per-asset checksum file was published with the release, making updates
impossible until checksums were configured.

Changes:
- If checksum is available: verify and show "Checksum verified ✓"
- If checksum is missing: warn with yellow message but continue update
- If checksum verification fails: clean up temp file, show detailed error
  with expected vs actual hash prefixes, then abort

This ensures the update mechanism works immediately for releases that
don't include checksums while still providing verification when available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
